story.title: Tracking Every Atom with Dr. Nair
story.text:
Hello! I am Dr. Priya Nair. I am a materials scientist at 3M. My job is to be a detective for matter. I track every single atom to make sure nothing gets lost when we make new things. This important rule is called the law of Conservation of matter.

Imagine we have an ice cube in a completely sealed jar. The first thing I measure is its total mass. This is our Starting Mass, and it is very important. It tells us how much 'stuff' is in our system.

Next, I check the Air Temperature around the jar. If the temperature gets warmer, the ice begins to melt. This is a Physical Change. The water is not disappearing. It is only changing its State of Matter from a solid to a liquid.

Inside the jar, the Ice Amount gets smaller. At the same time, the Liquid Water Amount gets bigger. But guess what? The total mass inside the jar never changes! It stays exactly the same.

My work is just like this, but with different materials. I have to think about other things too, like gases we can’t see. Does making a gas mean mass is lost? No! We just have to measure it, too.

margin_defs.definition: closed so nothing can get in or out
a very tiny piece of matter

check_prompts: What happens to the total mass when ice melts in a sealed jar?

vocabulary.definition: The rule that matter is never lost or made, only changed.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The law of conservation means the total mass always stays the same.

vocabulary.definition: The form of something, like a solid, liquid, or gas.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Ice and water are different states of matter.

vocabulary.definition: A change in how something looks or feels, but not what it is.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Melting an ice cube is a physical change.

vocabulary.definition: How much 'stuff' or matter is in an object.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The mass of the water is the same whether it is ice or liquid.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Nair tracks gases to make sure no mass is lost. What is an example of a gas you have seen or felt?
discussion_cards.frame: An example of a gas I have seen or felt is ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Why is it important for a company like 3M to not waste any matter when making products?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important to not waste matter because ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
